Commercial Mar 25, 2026

On March 25, 2026, Wizz Air announced that it had signed a deal with Barcelona-based inflight technology provider… The post Wizz Air COO on recovering the cost lead, offering Wi-Fi, and hedging fuel appeared first on AeroTime.

Wizz Air's Wi-Fi investment signals that ultra-low-cost carriers are now competing on amenities, not just fares, forcing industry recalibration of margin expectations.

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

House Moves to Bring Back Civil Overland Supersonic Flight

Measure would direct FAA to write new regulations for civil aircraft flying faster than Mach 1.

Congressional action to enable supersonic flight would require the FAA to revise 50-year-old noise restrictions, potentially opening a multi-billion-dollar market for aircraft manufacturers like Boom and Spike.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Make-Or-Break Week: United Airlines Enters Final Contract Talks With Flight Attendants

Representatives of the carrier's cabin crew are hoping to be able to lock down a tentative agreement.

United's flight attendant negotiations this week could set wage and work rule precedent affecting compensation expectations across major U.S. carriers.

See Simple Flying →

Drones Mar 25, 2026

DJI just nuked drone prices across the board

Something strange is happening to DJI drone prices right now. And if you’ve ever even briefly considered buying a drone, this is one of those moments where ignoring it might come back to haunt you. Because this isn’t a typical seasonal sale or a small round of discounts tucked away on a product p...

DJI's aggressive price reductions across its entire lineup signal intensifying competition in the consumer drone market, potentially reshaping margins and accessibility standards industry-wide.

See DroneDJ →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

The SMS Declaration of Compliance represents a mandatory shift toward proactive risk management systems rather than reactive accident investigation, requiring operators to document safety processes continuously.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Air Arabia debuts Panasonic Avionics multi-orbit IFC

Already a customer of Panasonic Avionics’ eXW wireless inflight entertainment system, UAE-based Air Arabia is now flying with the IFEC giant’s Ku-band multi-orbit onboard connectivity solution, Runway Girl Network can reveal. The all-Airbus A320 Family operator is among a cluster of carriers roll...

Air Arabia's adoption of Panasonic's multi-orbit Ku-band connectivity enables consistent global coverage beyond traditional single-satellite limitations, reducing coverage gaps for low-cost carriers on international routes.

See Runway Girl Network →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

FAA Letter Says Whiteman Airport Obligated to Stay Open

Los Angeles County still continues to study closure.

Despite LA County's ongoing closure study, the FAA's obligation letter creates legal barriers that could significantly delay or prevent Whiteman Airport's shutdown regardless of local preferences.

Philippine carriers cut flights, secure fuel as government declares emergency

The Philippines has become the first country to declare a national state of energy emergency amid escalating Middle… The post Philippine carriers cut flights, secure fuel as government declares emergency appeared first on AeroTime.

Asian carriers face operational disruptions as geopolitical energy crises force governments to implement emergency protocols that restrict fuel availability.

Safety Mar 25, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective safety risk management requires organizations to document why selected controls are adequate and justify acceptance of remaining risks, moving beyond theoretical analysis to defensible operational decisions.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Southwest Airlines Cabin Reconfiguration Increases Seat Pitch, Reducing Overall Passenger Capacity

Southwest Airlines has added extra-legroom seats to its fleet.

Southwest's capacity trade-off signals a strategic pivot toward premium revenue per seat over volume, potentially reshaping ultra-low-cost carrier economics.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

$15.8 Billion Vote Of Confidence: Airbus Scores Massive 101-Plane Deal

The European planemaker continues to make big steps forward in a key market.

Airbus's 101-plane deal signals sustained confidence in narrow-body aircraft demand despite economic uncertainties, strengthening its competitive position against Boeing in a critical market segment.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Why Can’t The Airbus A350 Be Powered By Any Other Engine Type?

The Airbus A350 and Rolls-Royce Trent XWB are a match made in aviation heaven.

The A350's airframe is exclusively optimized for the Trent XWB's architecture, making engine re-certification economically and technically prohibitive for airlines seeking alternatives.

See Simple Flying →

▶
Safety Mar 25, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter accident

Wire strike fatalities stem from pilots' dangerous overconfidence in reaction time rather than wire visibility, making pre-flight route planning and low-altitude awareness critical survival skills.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Safety Mar 25, 2026

JetBlue Flight Halted After Bizarre Runway Encounter With Coyote

The aircraft did not suffer any damage and was back in the air shortly after the incident, transporting passengers to their original destination.

Wildlife incursions at busy airports pose operational disruptions that ground crews must manage quickly to minimize passenger delays and revenue impact.

See Simple Flying →

Drones Mar 25, 2026

Warehouse drones are quietly transforming alcohol distribution across America

In a move that shows just how fast warehouse automation is evolving, Corvus Robotics has teamed up with Southern Glazer’s Wine & Spirits to scale autonomous inventory drones across one of the largest beverage distribution networks in the US. more…

Autonomous warehouse drones are now operationalizing at scale in complex logistics networks, signaling accelerated adoption of aerial robotics beyond research phases into mainstream supply chain operations.

See DroneDJ →

Drones Mar 25, 2026

Bay Area next as Wing expands drone delivery network

The Wing team has announced plans to expand its ultra-fast residential drone delivery service across the San Francisco Bay Area in the coming months, marking a full-circle moment for the company that was originally born inside Google X back in 2012. more…

Wing's Bay Area expansion signals mainstream adoption of autonomous drone delivery beyond initial markets, validating the commercial viability of last-mile logistics automation.

General Aviation Mar 25, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use t

Eastern Iowa Airport's diverse traffic mix from trainers to air carriers positions it as a critical regional hub balancing flight training operations with commercial service demands.

14+ Hours Nonstop: Delta Air Lines Reveals Brand New Long-Haul Route

Delta Air Lines has disclosed another new long-haul route: Los Angeles to Manila. While it has not been officially announced yet, it was revealed in the airline's response to Philippine Airlines' and its application to the US Department of Transportation to begin flights from Manila to Chicago O'...

Delta's LA-Manila route signals aggressive expansion into underserved Asia-Pacific markets amid competitive pressure from regional carriers seeking similar US gateway access.

Air Cargo Mar 25, 2026

Meet The New King Of Cargo: Atlas Air Orders 20 Airbus A350Fs

Well, it looks like in a few years’ time, the United States will have ANOTHER A350-operator alongside Delta… but it won’t be flying passengers! That’s because Airbus announced on March 16th that Atlas Air Worldwide has placed a firm order for 20 Airbus A350F freighters, making it the largest cust...

Atlas Air's A350F order signals a major shift toward modern wide-body cargo capacity, positioning the carrier to compete with established freight operators and potentially reshape air-cargo network economics.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Porter to launch 12 routes out of new Montreal Metropolitan Airport terminal

Porter Airlines will be the launch carrier at Montreal Metropolitan Airport (YHU) brand new terminal. Starting on June… The post Porter to launch 12 routes out of new Montreal Metropolitan Airport terminal appeared first on AeroTime.

Porter's expansion to a new airport terminal signals regional carrier confidence in underserved markets, potentially reshaping competitive dynamics in Eastern Canada's aviation landscape.

See AeroTime Hub →

Industry Mar 25, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization through technology integration offers airlines and airports a faster path to emissions reduction than waiting for next-generation aircraft development.

See MDPI →

Mass TSA resignations during shutdown could create critical security screening gaps that threaten airline operations and passenger throughput at major hubs.

Air France Has Ended Airbus A380 Flights On 18 Routes: Full List

Uncover the hidden gems among the Air France's A380 routes, including the five airports that only saw the carrier's superjumbos.

Air France's A380 retirement from 18 routes reveals shifting demand patterns where smaller widebody aircraft now offer better economics on medium-haul and lower-density long-haul services.

Military Mar 25, 2026

Photos of A-10s Used to Strike Iranian Naval Vessels Show Video Game-Inspired Nicknames

Images shared by U.S. Central Command suggest that at least some A-10s taking part in Operation Epic Fury carry new video game-inspired nicknames, an operationally irrelevant detail but one likely to interest spotters and modelers. As we already reported a few days ago, the U.S. Air Force A-10 Th...

A-10 Thunderbolt II aircraft involved in recent Iranian naval strikes display personalized video game-inspired nose art, reflecting pilot morale and unit identity practices that persist in modern combat operations.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Press Release: Immfly, Wizz Air, and gateretail partner on digital cabin

Immfly, Wizz Air, and gateretail have launched an integrated digital cabin ecosystem that unifies onboard retail, passenger and crew connectivity, and operational data into a single platform, marking a significant step forward in the digital transformation of Europe’s commercial aviation sector. ...

Unified digital platforms integrating retail, connectivity, and operations can reduce fragmentation costs and improve passenger experience across low-cost carrier fleets.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Long-haul flights for families may become a little easier in the future after United Airlines unveiled plans to… The post United Airlines eases long-haul travel for families with new economy beds appeared first on AeroTime.

United's economy bed product targets the underserved family long-haul segment, potentially pressuring competitors to enhance economy comfort offerings on international routes.

Military Mar 25, 2026

DOT&E Says No Combat-Capable TR-3-Configured F-35s Were Delivered in FY2025

The latest Director, Operational Test & Evaluation report says no combat-capable F-35 TR-3 aircraft have been delivered to the U.S. military until September 2025 because of the truncated software. The recently released 2025 Director, Operational Test & Evaluation (DOT&E) annual report brought ano...

Software delays are preventing the U.S. military from achieving full F-35 operational capability, creating a critical gap between aircraft delivery and combat readiness.

See The Aviationist →

Wizz Air's digital cabin integration with Immfly positions ultra-low-cost carriers to monetize ancillary services through connected passenger experiences, directly challenging full-service competitors' traditional revenue models.

The A321XLR enables carriers to profitably serve underutilized long-haul routes with lower capacity requirements, expanding international network connectivity without requiring widebody aircraft investment.

Drone enters Latvian airspace from Russia, crashes near Kraslava

Drones Mar 25, 2026

America’s new 911 drone is always ready

Drones responding to 911 calls are no longer experimental. Across the US, police departments are already using Drone as First Responder (DFR) programs to get eyes on emergencies faster than patrol units. In many cities, drones arrive in under two minutes, well before officers. But even with that ...

Drone-first response protocols are achieving sub-two-minute emergency arrivals, fundamentally accelerating situational awareness ahead of traditional ground units and reshaping operational dispatch prioritization.

See DroneDJ →

Drones Mar 25, 2026

DJI’s latest deals make shaky phone footage a thing of the past

Smartphones today can shoot cinematic 4K, handle low light, and even mimic DSLR depth. But there’s still one thing they can’t fix on their own: movement. That tiny shake when you walk. That awkward jitter when you pan. That “almost good” clip that never quite looks professional. That’s exactly wh...

DJI's stabilization technology addresses the final frontier in smartphone cinematography, positioning gimbal solutions as essential accessories for content creators rather than niche prosumer tools.

See DroneDJ →
